reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about the limited scope of the patch, specifically that it only works for zram and doesn't address other potential benefits of compressed writeback. Suggested exploring alternative approaches such as a small-slot allocator or writing back a full backing page.

reviews.author: Askar Safin
reviews.summary: Asked about the possibility of checksummed swap to protect against disk bit-rot and hibernation image corruption. Mentioned a previous attempt to use dm-integrity, but it's incompatible with mainline kernel.
reviews.raw_body:
Nhat Pham <nphamcs@gmail.com>:
> I'm aware of that work. It's an improvement, but my understanding is:

Thank you for answer!

Also, is it possible to have checksummed swap?

I want to have checksummed swap to be protected from disk bit-rot
(I already have ECC memory, so RAM is protected).

And hibernation image should be protected, too.

I tried to put swap on top of dm-integrity, but this is
incompatible with hibernation in mainline kernel.

-- 
Askar Safin
